step.name: sketch-workload
step.input: Producer rate + consumer cost + latency budget
step.action: Estimate workload characteristics. Identify parallelism opportunity (CPU-bound vs I/O-bound).

step.name: size-the-pool
step.action: Pick pool size from downstream resource ceiling (DB connections, HTTP target rate). For CPU-bound, use runtime.NumCPU(); for I/O-bound, multiply.

step.name: pick-pipeline-shape
step.action: Default to fan-out/fan-in: producer → bounded pool → fan-in collector. Bespoke shapes require ADR.

step.name: plumb-cancellation
step.action: Pass ctx through every goroutine. Each goroutine checks ctx.Done() in its loop. Use errgroup.WithContext to coordinate.
step.decision-gate: If cancellation cannot be wired (legacy API), fork a derived context with timeout as a fallback.

step.name: audit-for-leaks
step.action: Run pprof goroutine profile under sustained load. Confirm steady-state count. No growth = no leak.
